title: The Cost of College Education
content_en:
The cost of higher education has risen dramatically in recent decades, outpacing inflation and wage growth in many countries. This trend has made college increasingly unaffordable for many families and has sparked intense debate about the value and accessibility of higher education.

In the United States, the average cost of tuition and fees at a four-year public university has more than tripled over the past thirty years, even after adjusting for inflation. Private universities can cost upwards of fifty thousand dollars per year in tuition alone. These staggering figures have left many students and their families with difficult financial decisions.

Student loan debt has reached unprecedented levels, exceeding 1.7 trillion dollars in the United States alone. The burden of student loans can delay major life milestones such as homeownership, marriage, and having children. Some graduates spend decades paying off their educational debt, limiting their financial flexibility during their most productive years.

Various solutions have been proposed to address the college affordability crisis. These include increased public funding for higher education, income-based repayment plans, student loan forgiveness programs, and the expansion of low-cost community colleges. Some countries have eliminated tuition entirely for public universities, demonstrating that alternatives to the high-cost model exist.
content_cn:
近几十年来，高等教育成本急剧上升，超过了许多国家的通胀和工资增长。这一趋势使大学对许多家庭来说越来越负担不起。

在美国，四年制公立大学的平均学费在过去三十年中增长了两倍多。私立大学仅学费每年就可高达五万美元。

学生贷款债务已达到前所未有的水平，仅在美国就超过1.7万亿美元。学生贷款的负担可能推迟购房、结婚和生育等重大人生里程碑。

已经提出了各种解决方案来解决大学可负担性危机，包括增加公共资金、基于收入的还款计划、学生贷款减免计划等。

questions.option_b: Over 1.7 billion dollars

questions.explanation: 文章指出学生贷款的负担可能推迟购房、结婚和生育。

title: Student Mental Health
content_en:
The mental health of students has become an increasingly pressing concern in educational institutions worldwide. Rising rates of anxiety, depression, and stress among students of all ages have prompted calls for greater attention to psychological well-being as an integral component of education.

Multiple factors contribute to student mental health challenges. Academic pressure, social media use, financial stress, and uncertainty about the future all play significant roles. The transition to college can be particularly difficult, as students face new academic demands, social adjustments, and often their first experience of independent living.

Schools and universities are responding by expanding mental health services. Counseling centers, peer support programs, and wellness initiatives are becoming more common. Some institutions have implemented mandatory mental health education and training for faculty to help them recognize and respond to students in distress.

However, significant gaps remain. Many institutions lack adequate counseling resources, with long wait times for appointments. Stigma surrounding mental health issues persists, preventing some students from seeking help. Cultural differences in attitudes toward mental health can also affect students' willingness to access services. Addressing student mental health requires a comprehensive approach that involves not just treatment but also prevention and the creation of supportive learning environments.
content_cn:
学生的心理健康已成为全球教育机构日益紧迫的关注。焦虑、抑郁和压力的比例上升促使人们呼吁更多关注心理健康。

多种因素导致学生心理健康挑战。学术压力、社交媒体使用、经济压力和对未来的不确定性都起着重要作用。

学校和大学正在通过扩大心理健康服务来回应。咨询中心、同伴支持项目和健康倡议变得更加普遍。

然而，重大差距仍然存在。许多机构缺乏足够的咨询资源，预约等待时间很长。围绕心理健康问题的污名仍然存在。解决学生心理健康问题需要全面的方法。

title: International Student Exchange Programs
content_en:
International student exchange programs have become an increasingly popular component of higher education, offering students the opportunity to study in a foreign country for a semester or academic year. These programs provide academic, cultural, and personal benefits that can shape students' lives and careers.

Academically, studying abroad exposes students to different educational systems and approaches to learning. Students may encounter new perspectives on their field of study and gain access to resources and expertise not available at their home institutions. Many exchange programs also offer language immersion opportunities that dramatically improve foreign language proficiency.

The cultural benefits of studying abroad are equally significant. Living in a foreign country challenges students to step outside their comfort zones, adapt to new customs, and develop cross-cultural communication skills. These experiences foster greater cultural awareness, tolerance, and adaptability, qualities increasingly valued in a globalized workforce.

On a personal level, studying abroad promotes independence, self-confidence, and resilience. Navigating daily life in an unfamiliar environment builds problem-solving skills and self-reliance. Many students describe their study abroad experience as transformative, broadening their worldview and shaping their future aspirations. However, challenges include homesickness, culture shock, and financial barriers that can limit access for lower-income students.
content_cn:
国际学生交换项目已成为高等教育中越来越受欢迎的组成部分，为学生提供了在外国学习一个学期或一学年的机会。这些项目提供了可以塑造学生生活和职业的学术、文化和个人好处。

在学术上，留学使学生接触不同的教育系统和学习方法。许多交换项目还提供语言沉浸机会。

留学的文化效益同样显著。生活在异国挑战学生走出舒适区，适应新习俗，发展跨文化交流技能。

在个人层面上，留学促进独立性、自信和韧性。许多学生将留学经历描述为变革性的。然而，挑战包括思乡、文化冲击和财务障碍。

title: Education in the Developing World
content_en:
Access to quality education remains a significant challenge in many developing countries. While progress has been made in increasing enrollment rates, millions of children in developing regions still lack access to basic education, and the quality of available education often falls short of acceptable standards.

Infrastructure deficits pose a major obstacle. Many schools lack basic facilities such as electricity, clean water, and adequate sanitation. Classrooms may be overcrowded, with dozens of students sharing limited space and resources. In rural areas, children may need to travel long distances to reach the nearest school.

The shortage of qualified teachers is another critical challenge. Many developing countries lack sufficient teacher training programs, and teachers are often underpaid and overworked. In some regions, untrained volunteers or community members fill teaching positions out of necessity.

Despite these challenges, innovative approaches are making a difference. Mobile schools serve nomadic populations, radio and television programs deliver education to remote areas, and low-cost tablet computers are being used to supplement limited instructional resources. International organizations and governments are working together to improve educational outcomes, recognizing that education is essential for economic development and social progress.
content_cn:
在许多发展中国家，获得优质教育仍然是一个重大挑战。虽然在提高入学率方面取得了进展，但发展中国家仍有数百万儿童无法获得基础教育。

基础设施不足是一个主要障碍。许多学校缺乏电力、清洁水和适当的卫生设施等基本设施。教室可能过于拥挤。

合格教师短缺是另一个关键挑战。许多发展中国家缺乏足够的教师培训项目，教师往往薪酬过低、工作过度。

尽管存在这些挑战，创新方法正在发挥作用。流动学校为游牧人口服务，广播和电视节目为偏远地区提供教育，低成本平板电脑被用来补充有限的教学资源。

title: The Role of Parents in Education
content_en:
Parents play a crucial role in their children's educational success, a fact consistently supported by research. The involvement of parents in their children's learning, both at home and at school, significantly impacts academic achievement, attendance, behavior, and social development.

At home, parents create the foundation for learning through everyday activities. Reading to young children, discussing school topics at the dinner table, and providing a quiet space for homework all contribute to academic success. Parents who demonstrate positive attitudes toward education instill similar values in their children.

At school, parent involvement takes many forms. Attending parent-teacher conferences, volunteering in classrooms, and participating in school governance all strengthen the connection between home and school. When parents and teachers work as partners, students benefit from consistent expectations and support across both environments.

However, parent involvement is not equally distributed. Economic constraints, language barriers, work schedules, and cultural differences can limit some parents' ability to participate. Single parents and those working multiple jobs may find it particularly challenging to be involved. Schools are increasingly exploring flexible options such as virtual meetings and multilingual communications to engage a broader range of families.
content_cn:
父母在孩子的教育成功中扮演着关键角色，这一事实得到了研究的一贯支持。父母在家庭和学校参与孩子的学习，对学业成就、出勤、行为和社会发展都有重大影响。

在家里，父母通过日常活动为学习奠定基础。给孩子读书、在餐桌上讨论学校话题、提供安静的家庭作业空间都有助于学业成功。

在学校，父母参与有多种形式。参加家长会、在教室做志愿者、参与学校治理都加强了家庭和学校之间的联系。

然而，父母参与并非平等分布。经济限制、语言障碍、工作日程和文化差异可能限制一些父母的参与能力。学校正在探索灵活的选择来吸引更广泛的家庭参与。

title: Climate Change and Its Effects
content_en:
Climate change is one of the most pressing challenges facing humanity. The Earth's average temperature has risen by approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius since the pre-industrial era, primarily due to human activities that release greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. This seemingly small increase has already had significant consequences.

Rising sea levels threaten coastal communities worldwide. As polar ice caps and glaciers melt, the volume of ocean water increases, gradually inundating low-lying areas. Island nations such as the Maldives and Tuvalu face existential threats, while major coastal cities like Miami and Shanghai are already experiencing increased flooding.

Extreme weather events are becoming more frequent and severe. Heat waves, droughts, hurricanes, and wildfires are intensifying, causing devastating impacts on communities and ecosystems. The economic costs of climate-related disasters have skyrocketed, straining government budgets and insurance systems.

The impact on biodiversity is equally alarming. Many species are struggling to adapt to rapidly changing conditions. Coral reefs, which support roughly 25 percent of all marine species, are particularly vulnerable to warming ocean temperatures. International agreements like the Paris Agreement aim to limit global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius, but current commitments fall short of what is needed to achieve this target.
content_cn:
气候变化是人类面临的最紧迫挑战之一。自工业革命前时代以来，地球平均温度已上升约1.1摄氏度，主要是由于人类活动释放温室气体。这看似很小的增长已经产生了重大后果。

海平面上升威胁着全球沿海社区。随着极地冰盖和冰川融化，岛国如马尔代夫和图瓦卢面临生存威胁。

极端天气事件变得更加频繁和严重。热浪、干旱、飓风和野火正在加剧，对社区和生态系统造成毁灭性影响。

对生物多样性的影响同样令人担忧。许多物种正在努力适应迅速变化的条件。珊瑚礁尤其容易受到海洋变暖的影响，它们支持着大约25%的海洋物种。

title: Plastic Pollution in the Oceans
content_en:
Plastic pollution has emerged as one of the most severe environmental crises of our time. An estimated eight million metric tons of plastic waste enter the world's oceans every year, threatening marine ecosystems and the livelihoods of millions of people who depend on the sea.

The scale of the problem is staggering. The Great Pacific Garbage Patch, a massive accumulation of plastic debris in the Pacific Ocean, is now estimated to be twice the size of Texas. Microplastics, tiny plastic fragments less than five millimeters in length, have been found in every corner of the ocean, from the deepest trenches to the most remote Arctic ice.

Marine wildlife is particularly vulnerable to plastic pollution. Sea turtles mistake plastic bags for jellyfish, their primary food source, and often die from ingesting them. Seabirds feed plastic fragments to their chicks, and whales have been found with stomachs full of plastic waste. The impact extends throughout the food chain, as microplastics are consumed by fish and other organisms that eventually end up on human dinner plates.

Efforts to address plastic pollution include bans on single-use plastics, improved waste management systems, and innovative technologies for cleaning up ocean debris. However, the most effective solution is reducing plastic production and consumption at the source. Consumer behavior change, corporate responsibility, and government regulation all play essential roles in tackling this global crisis.
content_cn:
塑料污染已成为我们这个时代最严重的环境危机之一。估计每年有800万公吨塑料废物进入世界海洋。

问题的规模令人震惊。太平洋垃圾带估计有德克萨斯州面积的两倍大。微塑料已在海洋的每个角落被发现。

海洋野生动物特别容易受到塑料污染的伤害。海龟将塑料袋误认为水母，海鸟将塑料碎片喂给雏鸟，鲸鱼被发现胃里满是塑料废物。影响延伸到整个食物链。

解决塑料污染的努力包括禁止一次性塑料、改善废物管理系统和清理海洋垃圾的创新技术。然而，最有效的解决方案是从源头减少塑料生产和消费。

questions.option_a: The size of a football field

questions.option_c: They are attracted to plastic colors

questions.option_b: Reducing plastic production and consumption at the source
questions.explanation: 文章指出最有效的解决方案是从源头减少塑料生产和消费。

title: Deforestation and Its Consequences
content_en:
Deforestation, the large-scale removal of forests, is occurring at an alarming rate worldwide. The United Nations estimates that approximately 10 million hectares of forest are lost each year, an area roughly equivalent to the size of Iceland. This destruction has far-reaching consequences for the environment, biodiversity, and human communities.

Forests play a critical role in regulating the Earth's climate. They absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, acting as vital carbon sinks that help mitigate climate change. When forests are cleared or burned, this stored carbon is released back into the atmosphere, contributing to global warming. Deforestation accounts for roughly 10 percent of global greenhouse gas emissions.

The impact on biodiversity is devastating. Tropical rainforests, which cover less than 7 percent of the Earth's land surface, are home to more than half of all terrestrial species. As these forests are destroyed, countless species lose their habitats, and many face extinction. The loss of biodiversity reduces ecosystem resilience and eliminates potential sources of medicines and other valuable resources.

Indigenous communities that have lived in and depended on forests for generations are also severely affected. Deforestation destroys their homes, traditional ways of life, and cultural heritage. Efforts to combat deforestation include reforestation programs, sustainable forestry practices, and international agreements that provide financial incentives for preserving forests.
content_cn:
森林砍伐正在以惊人的速度在全球范围内发生。联合国估计每年大约有1000万公顷森林消失，面积大约相当于冰岛的大小。

森林在调节地球气候方面发挥着关键作用。它们吸收大气中的二氧化碳，作为重要的碳汇帮助减缓气候变化。森林砍伐约占全球温室气体排放的10%。

对生物多样性的影响是毁灭性的。热带雨林覆盖了不到7%的地球陆地表面，却是一半以上陆地物种的家园。

世世代代生活在森林中并依赖森林的原住民社区也受到严重影响。森林砍伐摧毁了他们的家园、传统生活方式和文化遗产。

questions.option_b: About 10 million hectares

questions.option_b: About 5%

questions.option_b: Less than 7%
questions.explanation: 文章指出热带雨林覆盖了不到7%的地球陆地表面。

questions.option_b: Make less severe or reduce

title: Water Scarcity: A Growing Crisis
content_en:
Water scarcity is rapidly becoming one of the most critical challenges of the twenty-first century. While water covers approximately 71 percent of the Earth's surface, only about 2.5 percent is freshwater, and much of that is locked in glaciers and ice caps. Less than 1 percent of the world's water is readily accessible for human use.

Currently, more than two billion people live in countries experiencing high water stress. By 2025, it is estimated that two-thirds of the world's population may face water shortages. Climate change is exacerbating the problem by altering precipitation patterns, increasing the frequency and severity of droughts, and reducing the availability of freshwater in many regions.

Agriculture is the largest consumer of freshwater, accounting for approximately 70 percent of global freshwater withdrawals. Inefficient irrigation practices waste enormous amounts of water, and water-intensive crops are often grown in arid regions. Industrial and domestic water use also contributes to scarcity, particularly in rapidly urbanizing areas.

Solutions to water scarcity include improving water use efficiency in agriculture through drip irrigation and drought-resistant crops, recycling and reusing wastewater, protecting watersheds and wetlands, and developing desalination technologies. Water pricing reforms that reflect the true value of water can also encourage conservation. Addressing water scarcity requires coordinated action at local, national, and international levels.
content_cn:
水资源短缺正在迅速成为二十一世纪最关键的挑战之一。虽然水覆盖了地球表面约71%，但只有约2.5%是淡水，其中大部分被锁在冰川和冰盖中。不到1%的世界水资源可供人类使用。

目前，超过20亿人生活在经历高水资源压力的国家。到2025年，估计三分之二的世界人口可能面临水资源短缺。

农业是最大的淡水消费者，约占全球淡水取水量的70%。低效的灌溉方式浪费了大量水。

解决水资源短缺的方案包括通过滴灌和抗旱作物提高农业用水效率、回收和再利用废水、保护流域和湿地、开发海水淡化技术。

questions.option_b: Over 2 billion

title: Endangered Species Conservation
content_en:
The alarming rate of species extinction has made endangered species conservation a critical priority for the global community. Scientists estimate that the current rate of extinction is between 1,000 and 10,000 times higher than the natural background rate, primarily due to human activities.

Habitat destruction is the leading cause of species endangerment. As human populations grow and expand, natural habitats are converted to agricultural land, urban areas, and industrial sites. Pollution, climate change, overexploitation, and invasive species also contribute to the decline of wildlife populations.

Conservation efforts take many forms. Protected areas such as national parks and wildlife reserves provide safe habitats for endangered species. Captive breeding programs aim to increase population numbers of critically endangered species with the goal of eventually reintroducing them into the wild. International agreements, such as the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species, regulate the trade of threatened animals and plants.

Success stories demonstrate that conservation can work. The giant panda, once on the brink of extinction, has seen its population increase thanks to habitat protection and breeding programs. The bald eagle recovered dramatically after the banning of the pesticide DDT. However, conservation requires sustained funding, political will, and public engagement to address the ongoing threats facing the world's biodiversity.
content_cn:
物种灭绝的惊人速度使濒危物种保护成为全球社会的关键优先事项。科学家估计当前灭绝速度是自然背景灭绝率的1000到10000倍，主要是由于人类活动。

栖息地破坏是物种濒危的主要原因。污染、气候变化、过度开发和入侵物种也导致野生动物种群下降。

保护努力有多种形式。国家公园和野生动物保护区等受保护区域为濒危物种提供安全栖息地。圈养繁殖计划旨在增加极度濒危物种的种群数量。

成功案例表明保护可以奏效。大熊猫曾经濒临灭绝，由于栖息地保护和繁殖计划，种群数量有所增加。白头鹰在禁止杀虫剂DDT后大幅恢复。

questions.option_b: 1,000-10,000 times higher
questions.explanation: 文章指出当前灭绝速度是自然背景灭绝率的1000到10000倍。

questions.question_text: What is the leading cause of species endangerment?
questions.option_b: Habitat destruction
questions.explanation: 文章指出栖息地破坏是物种濒危的主要原因。

questions.option_b: The giant panda population increase
questions.option_d: The elimination of all invasive species
questions.explanation: 文章指出大熊猫由于保护努力种群数量有所增加。

questions.question_text: The word 'overexploitation' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Using resources excessively to the point of depletion
questions.explanation: 'overexploitation'意为'过度开发'。

title: Air Pollution and Public Health
content_en:
Air pollution has become a major public health crisis, particularly in rapidly developing countries. The World Health Organization estimates that air pollution causes approximately seven million premature deaths annually, making it one of the leading environmental health risks worldwide.

The sources of air pollution are diverse. Vehicle emissions, industrial processes, power generation, and agricultural activities all contribute to poor air quality. In many developing cities, the rapid increase in motor vehicles, combined with limited emission controls, has created dangerously high levels of air pollution.

Particulate matter, especially fine particles known as PM2.5, poses the greatest health risk. These microscopic particles can penetrate deep into the lungs and enter the bloodstream, causing respiratory diseases, cardiovascular problems, and lung cancer. Children, the elderly, and those with pre-existing health conditions are particularly vulnerable.

The economic impact of air pollution is substantial. Healthcare costs, lost productivity, and reduced quality of life impose significant burdens on societies. Many cities are taking action by promoting public transportation, implementing vehicle emission standards, and transitioning to cleaner energy sources. Individual actions such as reducing energy consumption and using sustainable transportation also contribute to cleaner air.
content_cn:
空气污染已成为一个重大的公共健康危机，特别是在快速发展中国家。世界卫生组织估计空气污染每年造成约700万人过早死亡。

空气污染的来源多种多样。车辆排放、工业过程、发电和农业活动都导致空气质量差。PM2.5等细颗粒物造成最大的健康风险。

这些微小颗粒可以深入肺部并进入血液，导致呼吸系统疾病、心血管问题和肺癌。儿童、老年人和有既往健康状况的人特别容易受到伤害。

空气污染的经济影响是巨大的。许多城市正在采取行动，推广公共交通、实施车辆排放标准和转向更清洁的能源。

questions.option_b: Approximately 7 million

questions.option_b: Carbon dioxide

questions.option_b: Children, the elderly, and those with pre-existing conditions

title: Sustainable Agriculture
content_en:
Sustainable agriculture seeks to meet society's food needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. As the global population approaches 10 billion by 2050, the challenge of producing enough food sustainably has never been more urgent.

Conventional farming practices have significant environmental impacts. Heavy use of chemical fertilizers and pesticides contaminates water supplies and degrades soil health. Monoculture farming, growing a single crop over large areas year after year, depletes soil nutrients and increases vulnerability to pests and diseases. Industrial livestock operations generate enormous amounts of waste and greenhouse gas emissions.

Sustainable agriculture offers alternative approaches. Crop rotation and diversification improve soil health and reduce pest problems naturally. Organic farming eliminates synthetic chemicals, though it typically produces lower yields. Precision agriculture uses technology to apply water, fertilizers, and pesticides only where needed, reducing waste and environmental impact.

Agroforestry, integrating trees into agricultural landscapes, provides multiple benefits including shade, wind protection, and additional income from fruit or timber. Regenerative agriculture goes beyond sustainability, aiming to restore degraded soils and sequester carbon. While transitioning to sustainable practices can be challenging and costly for farmers, the long-term benefits for food security and environmental health are substantial.
content_cn:
可持续农业试图在不损害后代满足自身需求能力的情况下满足社会的食物需求。到2050年全球人口接近100亿时，可持续生产足够食物的挑战比以往任何时候都更加紧迫。

传统农业做法对环境有重大影响。大量使用化学肥料和农药污染了水源并损害了土壤健康。单一作物种植消耗土壤养分并增加了病虫害的脆弱性。

可持续农业提供替代方法。轮作和多样化自然地改善土壤健康并减少虫害问题。有机农业消除了合成化学物质。精准农业使用技术仅在需要的地方施用水、肥料和农药。

农林业将树木整合到农业景观中。再生农业超越了可持续性，旨在恢复退化的土壤和固碳。

questions.option_b: Capture and store

title: Ocean Acidification
content_en:
Ocean acidification is often called the evil twin of climate change, and for good reason. As the oceans absorb approximately 30 percent of the carbon dioxide released into the atmosphere by human activities, the chemistry of seawater changes, becoming more acidic. Since the beginning of the industrial era, the pH of ocean surface water has decreased by 0.1 units, representing a 26 percent increase in acidity.

This seemingly small change has significant biological consequences. Many marine organisms, including corals, shellfish, and certain plankton, rely on calcium carbonate to build their shells and skeletons. As ocean acidity increases, the availability of carbonate ions decreases, making it more difficult for these organisms to build and maintain their protective structures.

Coral reefs are particularly vulnerable. Already stressed by rising water temperatures, acidification adds another threat to these vital ecosystems. If current trends continue, scientists project that coral reefs could dissolve faster than they can grow by the end of this century. The loss of coral reefs would be catastrophic, as they support approximately 25 percent of all marine species and provide goods and services valued at hundreds of billions of dollars annually.

Addressing ocean acidification ultimately requires reducing carbon dioxide emissions, the same action needed to combat climate change. Local measures such as reducing nutrient pollution and protecting marine habitats can help build resilience, but the root cause must be addressed at the global level.
content_cn:
海洋酸化常被称为气候变化的邪恶双胞胎。随着海洋吸收人类活动释放到大气中的约30%的二氧化碳，海水的化学成分发生变化，变得更加酸性。自工业时代开始以来，海洋表面水的pH值下降了0.1个单位，代表酸度增加了26%。

这看似微小的变化具有重大的生物学后果。许多海洋生物，包括珊瑚、贝类和某些浮游生物，依赖碳酸钙来构建它们的壳和骨骼。随着海洋酸度增加，碳酸根离子的可用性降低。

珊瑚礁特别脆弱。如果当前趋势继续，科学家预测到本世纪末珊瑚礁可能溶解得比生长还快。珊瑚礁的丧失将是灾难性的，因为它们支持约25%的海洋物种。

解决海洋酸化最终需要减少二氧化碳排放。减少营养物污染和保护海洋栖息地等地方措施可以帮助建立韧性。

questions.question_text: What percentage of CO2 released by human activities do oceans absorb?
questions.option_b: About 30%
questions.explanation: 文章指出海洋吸收约30%的二氧化碳。

questions.question_text: By how much has the pH of ocean surface water decreased since the industrial era?
questions.option_b: 0.1 units
questions.explanation: 文章指出pH值下降了0.1个单位。

questions.question_text: What percentage increase in acidity does the pH change represent?
questions.explanation: 文章指出pH变化代表酸度增加了26%。

questions.question_text: Why are coral reefs particularly vulnerable to acidification?
questions.option_a: They don't live in the ocean
questions.option_b: They rely on calcium carbonate which becomes harder to form in acidic water
questions.explanation: 文章指出珊瑚依赖碳酸钙构建结构，而酸度增加使碳酸根离子可用性降低。

title: Waste Management and Recycling
content_en:
The world generates an astonishing amount of waste. The World Bank estimates that global waste production will increase by 70 percent from 2016 levels by 2050, reaching 3.4 billion tons annually. Managing this waste sustainably is one of the greatest environmental challenges of our time.

Landfills remain the most common method of waste disposal worldwide, but they pose significant environmental problems. Decomposing organic waste produces methane, a greenhouse gas 25 times more potent than carbon dioxide. Leachate, a toxic liquid formed as waste breaks down, can contaminate groundwater and soil.

Recycling offers a more sustainable alternative by converting waste materials into new products. It conserves natural resources, reduces energy consumption, and decreases the amount of waste sent to landfills. However, recycling rates remain disappointingly low in many countries, and contamination of recyclable materials reduces the effectiveness of recycling programs.

The concept of a circular economy, which aims to eliminate waste entirely by designing products for reuse, repair, and recycling, is gaining traction. Countries like Japan and Germany have implemented comprehensive waste management systems that prioritize reduction, reuse, and recycling over disposal. Individual actions such as composting food waste and choosing products with minimal packaging also contribute to reducing the waste stream.
content_cn:
世界产生了惊人数量的废物。世界银行估计到2050年全球废物产量将从2016年水平增加70%，达到每年34亿吨。

垃圾填埋仍然是全球最常见的废物处理方法，但它们造成重大环境问题。分解的有机废物产生甲烷，这是一种比二氧化碳强25倍的温室气体。

回收通过将废物材料转化为新产品提供了更可持续的替代方案。它节约自然资源，减少能源消耗。然而，许多国家的回收率仍然低得令人失望。

循环经济的概念正在获得关注，旨在通过设计可重复使用、修理和回收的产品来完全消除废物。日本和德国等国家已经实施了全面的废物管理系统。

questions.option_b: 70% from 2016 levels
questions.explanation: 文章指出全球废物产量将从2016年水平增加70%。

questions.question_text: How much more potent is methane than carbon dioxide as a greenhouse gas?
questions.option_b: 10 times more potent
questions.explanation: 文章指出甲烷是比二氧化碳强25倍的温室气体。

questions.question_text: What is the concept of a circular economy?
questions.option_a: An economy that produces as much waste as possible
questions.option_b: An economy that aims to eliminate waste by designing products for reuse and recycling
questions.option_c: An economy based only on recycling
questions.explanation: 文章指出循环经济旨在通过设计可重复使用和回收的产品来完全消除废物。

questions.option_b: A toxic liquid formed as waste breaks down
questions.explanation: 'leachate'意为'渗滤液'，是废物分解过程中产生的有毒液体。

title: Renewable Energy and the Environment
content_en:
The transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy sources is essential for protecting the environment and combating climate change. Solar, wind, hydroelectric, and geothermal energy offer cleaner alternatives to coal, oil, and natural gas, producing little to no greenhouse gas emissions during operation.

Solar energy has experienced remarkable growth as panel costs have plummeted. Large-scale solar farms can now generate electricity at costs competitive with fossil fuels. Wind energy has similarly expanded, with both onshore and offshore wind farms contributing increasing shares of electricity generation in many countries.

However, renewable energy is not without environmental impacts. Large hydroelectric dams can disrupt river ecosystems and displace communities. Wind turbines can pose risks to birds and bats. Solar farms require significant land area, potentially conflicting with agricultural use or wildlife habitat. The mining of rare earth minerals needed for solar panels and wind turbines raises environmental and ethical concerns.

Despite these challenges, the environmental benefits of renewable energy far outweigh its impacts compared to fossil fuels. A comprehensive approach that combines renewable energy development with environmental protection, community engagement, and careful site planning can minimize negative effects while maximizing the benefits of the clean energy transition.
content_cn:
从化石燃料向可再生能源的转型对于保护环境和应对气候变化至关重要。太阳能、风能、水力发电和地热能提供了更清洁的替代方案。

太阳能经历了显著增长。大型太阳能农场现在可以以与化石燃料竞争的成本发电。风能同样扩大了。

然而，可再生能源并非没有环境影响。大型水电站可能破坏河流生态系统。风力涡轮机可能对鸟类构成风险。太阳能农场需要大量土地。太阳能电池板和风力涡轮机所需的稀土矿物开采引发了环境和道德担忧。

尽管存在这些挑战，可再生能源的环境效益远大于其影响。

questions.question_text: What is essential for protecting the environment according to the passage?
questions.option_b: Transitioning from fossil fuels to renewable energy
questions.explanation: 文章指出从化石燃料向可再生能源的转型对于保护环境至关重要。

questions.question_text: What environmental impact of wind turbines is mentioned?
questions.option_b: They can pose risks to birds and bats
questions.explanation: 文章指出风力涡轮机可能对鸟类和蝙蝠构成风险。

questions.question_text: What concern about solar panels and wind turbines is raised?
questions.option_b: Mining of rare earth minerals raises environmental and ethical concerns
questions.explanation: 文章指出稀土矿物开采引发了环境和道德担忧。

questions.question_text: The word 'plummeted' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Decreased rapidly
questions.explanation: 'plummeted'意为'急剧下降'。

title: Urban Green Spaces
content_en:
Urban green spaces, including parks, gardens, and tree-lined streets, play a vital role in making cities livable and sustainable. As the world becomes increasingly urbanized, with nearly 70 percent of the global population expected to live in cities by 2050, the importance of green spaces in urban environments cannot be overstated.

The environmental benefits of urban green spaces are numerous. Trees and plants absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen, improving air quality. Green spaces reduce the urban heat island effect, where cities experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to concrete and asphalt absorbing and retaining heat. Parks and gardens also help manage stormwater, reducing the risk of flooding.

The health benefits are equally important. Access to green spaces has been linked to reduced stress, improved mental health, and increased physical activity. Studies show that people who live near parks and green areas report higher levels of well-being and lower rates of cardiovascular disease.

However, urban green spaces face growing threats. Property development pressures often lead to the conversion of green spaces into buildings and infrastructure. In many fast-growing cities, green spaces are being lost faster than they are being created. Ensuring equitable access to green spaces is also a concern, as low-income neighborhoods often have less green space than affluent areas. Urban planning that prioritizes green infrastructure is essential for creating healthy, sustainable cities.
content_cn:
城市绿地，包括公园、花园和绿树成荫的街道，在使城市宜居和可持续方面发挥着重要作用。到2050年，全球近70%的人口预计将居住在城市中，城市环境中绿地的重要性不容忽视。

城市绿地的环境效益众多。树木和植物吸收二氧化碳并产生氧气，改善空气质量。绿地减少了城市热岛效应。公园和花园还有助于管理雨水，减少洪水风险。

健康益处同样重要。接触绿地已被证明可以减少压力、改善心理健康和增加体育活动。

然而，城市绿地面临日益增长的威胁。房地产开发压力常常导致绿地被转化为建筑和基础设施。确保公平获取绿地也是一个关注点，因为低收入社区通常比富裕地区的绿地更少。

questions.question_text: What percentage of the global population is expected to live in cities by 2050?
questions.option_b: About 70%

title: Protecting Marine Ecosystems
content_en:
Marine ecosystems, covering more than 70 percent of the Earth's surface, are among the most productive and diverse environments on the planet. They provide essential services including food, oxygen production, climate regulation, and recreation. However, these vital ecosystems face unprecedented threats from human activities.

Overfishing has depleted fish stocks worldwide. Approximately one-third of global fish stocks are currently fished at unsustainable levels. Destructive fishing practices such as bottom trawling damage seafloor habitats, while bycatch, the unintentional capture of non-target species, kills millions of marine animals annually.

Pollution from land-based sources is another major threat. Agricultural runoff carrying fertilizers creates dead zones where oxygen levels are too low to support marine life. Oil spills, while less frequent than in the past, continue to cause devastating damage when they occur. Noise pollution from shipping and naval activities disrupts marine mammals that rely on sound for communication and navigation.

Marine protected areas are one of the most effective tools for conservation. By restricting or prohibiting extractive activities within designated zones, these areas allow ecosystems to recover and thrive. Currently, only about 7 percent of the ocean is protected, well below the 30 percent target recommended by scientists. Expanding marine protected areas and enforcing existing regulations are critical steps toward preserving ocean health.
content_cn:
海洋生态系统覆盖了地球表面70%以上，是地球上最具生产力和多样性的环境之一。然而，这些重要生态系统面临着人类活动前所未有的威胁。

过度捕捞已使全球鱼类资源枯竭。约三分之一的全球鱼类资源目前以不可持续的水平被捕捞。破坏性捕捞做法如底拖网损害了海底栖息地。

来自陆地的污染是另一个主要威胁。携带肥料的农业径流造成了氧气水平太低无法支持海洋生物的死区。石油泄漏继续造成毁灭性损害。

海洋保护区是最有效的保护工具之一。目前只有约7%的海洋受到保护，远低于科学家推荐的30%目标。

title: The Impact of Fast Fashion
content_en:
The fast fashion industry has revolutionized the way people buy and discard clothing, but its environmental impact is devastating. Fast fashion refers to the rapid production of inexpensive clothing that reflects current trends, encouraging consumers to buy more and wear items fewer times before discarding them.

The fashion industry is responsible for approximately 10 percent of global carbon emissions, more than international flights and maritime shipping combined. Producing a single cotton t-shirt requires about 2,700 liters of water, enough to meet one person's drinking needs for two and a half years. The industry is also the second-largest consumer of water worldwide.

Textile waste is a growing problem. The average American throws away approximately 80 pounds of clothing each year, and the vast majority ends up in landfills or incinerators. Synthetic fabrics such as polyester shed microplastics when washed, contributing to the growing microplastic pollution in oceans and waterways.

Chemical pollution from textile dyeing and treatment is another serious concern. The fashion industry uses thousands of different chemicals, many of them toxic, in the production process. In countries with weak environmental regulations, factory wastewater is often discharged directly into rivers, contaminating water supplies and harming aquatic life. Sustainable fashion movements are encouraging consumers to buy less, choose quality over quantity, and support ethical brands.
content_cn:
快时尚行业彻底改变了人们购买和丢弃服装的方式，但其环境影响是毁灭性的。快时尚指的是快速生产反映当前潮流的廉价服装。

时尚行业约占全球碳排放的10%，比国际航班和海运的总和还多。生产一件棉质T恤需要约2700升水。该行业也是全球第二大水资源消耗者。

纺织品废物是一个日益严重的问题。美国人平均每年扔掉约80磅服装，绝大多数最终进入垃圾填埋场。合成纤维如聚酯在洗涤时会脱落微塑料。

纺织品染色和处理产生的化学污染是另一个严重问题。可持续时尚运动正在鼓励消费者少买、注重质量而非数量。

title: Biodiversity Loss
content_en:
Biodiversity, the variety of life on Earth, is declining at an unprecedented rate. The Intergovernmental Science-Policy Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services reported in 2019 that approximately one million species face extinction, many within decades. This represents the fastest decline in biodiversity in human history.

The loss of biodiversity threatens the ecosystem services that human civilization depends upon. Pollinators such as bees and butterflies are essential for food production, with approximately 75 percent of global food crops relying to some degree on animal pollination. The decline in pollinator populations poses a direct threat to food security.

Healthy ecosystems also provide water purification, flood protection, carbon sequestration, and disease regulation. The degradation of these natural systems increases human vulnerability to natural disasters, water scarcity, and disease outbreaks. The economic value of ecosystem services has been estimated at trillions of dollars annually.

Addressing biodiversity loss requires transforming how we produce food, manage lands, and build cities. Expanding and connecting protected areas, restoring degraded ecosystems, and integrating biodiversity considerations into economic decision-making are essential strategies. The recently adopted Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework aims to protect 30 percent of the Earth's land and ocean by 2030, an ambitious but necessary target.
content_cn:
生物多样性，即地球上生命的多样性，正以前所未有的速度下降。2019年报告指出约100万种物种面临灭绝。这代表了人类历史上生物多样性最快的下降。

生物多样性的丧失威胁着人类文明所依赖的生态系统服务。传粉昆虫如蜜蜂和蝴蝶对粮食生产至关重要，约75%的全球粮食作物在某种程度上依赖动物传粉。传粉昆虫种群的下降直接威胁粮食安全。

健康的生态系统还提供水净化、防洪、固碳和疾病调节等服务。这些自然系统的退化增加了人类对自然灾害、水资源短缺和疾病爆发的脆弱性。

解决生物多样性丧失需要转变我们生产食物、管理土地和建设城市的方式。最近通过的昆明-蒙特利尔全球生物多样性框架旨在到2030年保护30%的地球陆地和海洋。

title: The Importance of Wetlands
content_en:
Wetlands are among the most productive and valuable ecosystems on Earth, yet they are disappearing at an alarming rate. Approximately 35 percent of the world's wetlands have been lost since 1970, with the rate of loss accelerating in recent decades.

Wetlands provide numerous critical services. They act as natural water filters, trapping sediments and absorbing pollutants before water reaches rivers and oceans. They serve as natural flood buffers, absorbing and slowly releasing excess water during storms and heavy rainfall. Wetlands also store vast amounts of carbon in their soils, helping to regulate the global climate.

The biodiversity supported by wetlands is remarkable. Although they cover only about 6 percent of the Earth's land surface, wetlands are home to 40 percent of all species. They serve as crucial breeding grounds for fish, nesting sites for birds, and habitats for countless other organisms.

Despite their importance, wetlands face numerous threats. Drainage for agriculture and urban development, pollution, invasive species, and climate change all contribute to wetland degradation. The Ramsar Convention, an international treaty adopted in 1971, aims to conserve wetlands of international importance. However, enforcement remains a challenge, and many wetlands continue to be lost or degraded. Restoring damaged wetlands and protecting existing ones are essential for maintaining the ecological services they provide.
content_cn:
湿地是地球上最具生产力和价值的生态系统之一，但它们正在以惊人的速度消失。自1970年以来，约35%的世界湿地已经消失。

湿地提供许多关键服务。它们充当天然水过滤器，在水到达河流和海洋之前拦截沉积物和吸收污染物。它们作为天然洪水缓冲区。湿地还在其土壤中储存大量碳，帮助调节全球气候。

湿地支持的生物多样性是显著的。虽然它们仅覆盖约6%的地球陆地表面，却是40%物种的家园。

尽管湿地很重要，但面临许多威胁。农业和城市发展的排水、污染、入侵物种和气候变化都导致湿地退化。1971年通过的拉姆萨尔公约旨在保护具有国际重要性的湿地。

questions.explanation: 文章指出自1970年以来约35%的世界湿地已经消失。

questions.option_b: About 6%

title: Sustainable Urban Development
content_en:
As the world becomes increasingly urbanized, the concept of sustainable urban development has become crucial. By 2050, nearly 70 percent of the global population is expected to live in urban areas, placing enormous pressure on infrastructure, resources, and the environment.

Sustainable cities aim to meet the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations. This involves balancing economic growth, social equity, and environmental protection. Key principles include compact urban design, efficient public transportation, renewable energy use, and waste reduction.

Transportation is a critical component of sustainable urban development. Car-dependent cities generate high levels of pollution and congestion. In contrast, cities with comprehensive public transit systems, cycling infrastructure, and walkable neighborhoods produce fewer emissions and offer better quality of life. Cities like Copenhagen and Singapore are recognized as leaders in sustainable transportation.

Green building practices also contribute significantly to urban sustainability. Energy-efficient buildings reduce electricity consumption and greenhouse gas emissions. Green roofs and walls provide insulation, absorb rainwater, and reduce the urban heat island effect. Water conservation systems, including rainwater harvesting and greywater recycling, help cities manage water resources more efficiently. Creating truly sustainable cities requires integrated planning that considers the complex interactions between transportation, energy, housing, and natural systems.
content_cn:
随着世界日益城市化，可持续城市发展的概念变得至关重要。到2050年，全球近70%的人口预计将居住在城市地区。

可持续城市旨在满足当前需求而不损害后代的能力。关键原则包括紧凑的城市设计、高效的公共交通、可再生能源使用和废物减少。

交通是可持续城市发展的关键组成部分。依赖汽车的城市产生大量污染和拥堵。相比之下，拥有全面公共交通系统、自行车基础设施和适宜步行社区的城市排放更少。

绿色建筑实践也对城市可持续性做出重大贡献。节能建筑减少电力消耗和温室气体排放。绿色屋顶和墙壁提供隔热、吸收雨水和减少城市热岛效应。

questions.option_b: About 70%

title: Noise Pollution
content_en:
Noise pollution is an often overlooked but serious environmental issue that affects millions of people worldwide. Defined as unwanted or excessive sound that can have harmful effects on human health and the environment, noise pollution has become a growing concern in urban areas.

The primary sources of noise pollution include road traffic, aircraft, industrial activities, construction, and loud music. In major cities, noise levels regularly exceed recommended limits. The World Health Organization recommends that environmental noise should not exceed 55 decibels during the day and 40 decibels at night, but many urban residents are exposed to levels far above these thresholds.

The health impacts of chronic noise exposure are significant. Prolonged exposure to high noise levels can cause hearing loss, sleep disturbance, cardiovascular disease, and increased stress levels. Children exposed to chronic noise may experience difficulties with concentration and learning, affecting their academic performance.

Wildlife is also affected by noise pollution. Marine mammals that rely on sound for communication and navigation are particularly vulnerable to underwater noise from shipping and naval sonar. Birds may alter their songs or abandon noisy habitats, affecting their breeding success. Reducing noise pollution requires a combination of regulations, urban planning, technological solutions such as quieter vehicles, and individual awareness of the impact of noise on others.
content_cn:
噪音污染是一个经常被忽视但严重的环境问题，影响着全球数百万人。噪音污染被定义为可能对人类健康和环境产生有害影响的不需要或过度的声音。

噪音污染的主要来源包括道路交通、飞机、工业活动、建筑和嘈杂的音乐。世界卫生组织建议环境噪音白天不应超过55分贝，夜间不应超过40分贝，但许多城市居民暴露在远高于这些阈值的水平中。

长期噪音暴露的健康影响是显著的。长期暴露在高噪音水平中可能导致听力损失、睡眠障碍、心血管疾病和压力水平增加。

野生动物也受到噪音污染的影响。依赖声音进行交流的海洋哺乳动物特别容易受到船舶和水下声纳噪音的影响。

questions.option_b: 55 decibels
questions.explanation: 文章指出WHO建议白天环境噪音不应超过55分贝。

questions.question_text: What health impact of chronic noise exposure is mentioned?
questions.option_b: Hearing loss, sleep disturbance, cardiovascular disease, and increased stress
questions.explanation: 文章指出长期噪音暴露可导致听力损失、睡眠障碍、心血管疾病和压力增加。

questions.option_b: They may experience difficulties with concentration and learning

title: Environmental Impact of Mining
content_en:
Mining is essential for modern civilization, providing the raw materials needed for construction, manufacturing, and technology. However, the environmental impacts of mining can be severe and long-lasting, affecting land, water, air, and biodiversity.

One of the most visible impacts is the physical alteration of landscapes. Open-pit mining removes entire mountaintops and creates massive craters. The waste rock and tailings produced by mining operations can cover vast areas, destroying natural habitats. Even after mines are abandoned, the environmental damage often persists for decades or centuries.

Water pollution is a particularly serious concern. Mining operations can contaminate surface water and groundwater with heavy metals, chemicals, and acid mine drainage. Acid mine drainage occurs when sulfide minerals exposed during mining react with water and oxygen, producing sulfuric acid that can leach toxic metals from surrounding rock. This contaminated water can poison aquatic ecosystems and render water unsafe for human use.

Air pollution from mining includes dust, diesel emissions from heavy equipment, and emissions from smelting operations. These pollutants can affect the health of nearby communities and contribute to regional air quality problems.

Reclamation of mined lands is required in many countries, but the effectiveness varies. Restoring ecosystems to their pre-mining condition is often impossible, though some level of ecological recovery can be achieved with careful planning and long-term commitment.
content_cn:
采矿对现代文明至关重要，但其对环境的影响可能严重且持久。

最明显的影响之一是景观的物理改变。露天采矿移除了整个山顶并产生巨大的坑洞。采矿作业产生的废石和尾矿可以覆盖大片区域。

水污染是特别严重的担忧。采矿作业可能用重金属、化学物质和酸性矿山排水污染地表水和地下水。酸性矿山排水发生在采矿过程中暴露的硫化矿物与水和氧气反应时。

采矿造成的空气污染包括粉尘、重型设备的柴油排放和冶炼作业的排放。这些污染物可能影响附近社区的健康。

许多国家要求对采矿土地进行复垦，但效果各异。将生态系统恢复到采矿前的状况通常是不可能的。

questions.question_text: What is acid mine drainage?
questions.option_b: Sulfuric acid produced when sulfide minerals react with water and oxygen
questions.explanation: 文章指出酸性矿山排水是硫化矿物与水和氧气反应产生硫酸。

questions.question_text: What water pollution concern is associated with mining?
questions.option_b: Contamination with heavy metals, chemicals, and acid mine drainage
questions.explanation: 文章指出采矿可能用重金属、化学物质和酸性矿山排水污染水源。

questions.question_text: What is required in many countries after mining?
questions.option_b: Reclamation of mined lands
questions.explanation: 文章指出许多国家要求对采矿土地进行复垦。

questions.question_text: The word 'reclamation' most likely means:
questions.option_b: The process of restoring land to a useful condition
questions.explanation: 'reclamation'意为'复垦、恢复'，指将土地恢复到有用状态。

title: The Impact of Tourism on the Environment
content_en:
Tourism is one of the world's largest industries, employing hundreds of millions of people and generating trillions of dollars in economic activity. However, the environmental impact of tourism, particularly in sensitive ecosystems, has become a growing concern.

The carbon footprint of tourism is substantial. Air travel alone accounts for approximately 2.5 percent of global carbon emissions, and the tourism sector as a whole is responsible for about 8 percent of global emissions when accounting for transportation, accommodation, and activities. Cruise ships produce enormous quantities of waste and emissions relative to the number of passengers they carry.

Overtourism, the phenomenon of too many visitors overwhelming a destination, has become a major issue in popular tourist spots. Venice, Barcelona, and Machu Picchu are among the destinations that have struggled to manage visitor numbers. Overtourism leads to environmental degradation, strain on local infrastructure, and diminished quality of life for residents.

Ecotourism, which aims to minimize environmental impact while supporting conservation and local communities, offers a more sustainable alternative. However, the line between ecotourism and greenwashing can be thin, and some operations marketed as eco-friendly still cause significant environmental damage. Truly sustainable tourism requires careful management, visitor education, and a commitment to preserving the natural and cultural heritage that makes destinations attractive in the first place.
content_cn:
旅游业是世界上最大的行业之一，但旅游业对环境的影响已引起越来越多的关注。

旅游业的碳足迹巨大。仅航空旅行就占全球碳排放的约2.5%，整个旅游业约占全球排放的8%。邮轮相对于乘客数量产生大量废物和排放。

过度旅游，即过多游客使目的地不堪重负的现象，已成为热门旅游点的主要问题。威尼斯、巴塞罗那和马丘比丘都在努力管理游客数量。

生态旅游旨在最小化环境影响同时支持保护和当地社区，提供了更可持续的替代方案。然而，生态旅游和漂绿之间的界限可能很模糊。真正可持续的旅游需要仔细管理、游客教育和保护自然文化遗产的承诺。

questions.question_text: What percentage of global carbon emissions does air travel account for?
questions.option_b: About 2.5%
questions.explanation: 文章指出航空旅行约占全球碳排放的2.5%。

questions.question_text: What is the tourism sector's total share of global emissions?
questions.option_b: About 8%
questions.explanation: 文章指出整个旅游业约占全球排放的8%。

questions.question_text: What is overtourism?
questions.option_b: The phenomenon of too many visitors overwhelming a destination
questions.explanation: 文章指出过度旅游是过多游客使目的地不堪重负的现象。

questions.question_text: What does 'greenwashing' most likely mean?
questions.option_b: Misleading claims about environmental practices to appear eco-friendly
questions.explanation: 'greenwashing'意为'漂绿'，指误导性地声称环保以显得生态友好。

title: Environmental Impact of Food Production
content_en:
The global food system has an enormous environmental footprint. Agriculture occupies approximately 40 percent of the world's land area, uses about 70 percent of freshwater withdrawals, and is responsible for roughly a quarter of greenhouse gas emissions. As the global population grows and diets shift toward more resource-intensive foods, these impacts are expected to increase.

Livestock production is particularly resource-intensive. Producing one kilogram of beef requires approximately 15,000 liters of water, compared to about 300 liters for one kilogram of vegetables. Livestock also produces significant methane emissions, a greenhouse gas much more potent than carbon dioxide. The expansion of agricultural land for livestock feed is a major driver of deforestation, particularly in the Amazon region.

Food waste is another critical issue. Approximately one-third of all food produced globally is lost or wasted. When food ends up in landfills, it decomposes and releases methane. The resources used to produce wasted food, including water, energy, and land, are also squandered.

Solutions include shifting toward plant-based diets, improving agricultural efficiency, reducing food waste, and developing more sustainable farming practices. Technologies such as vertical farming, lab-grown meat, and precision agriculture offer promising pathways. Consumer choices play a crucial role, as demand drives production practices.
content_cn:
全球食品系统具有巨大的环境足迹。农业占据约40%的世界土地面积，使用约70%的淡水取水量，约占温室气体排放的四分之一。

畜牧业特别消耗资源。生产一公斤牛肉需要约15000升水，而一公斤蔬菜只需约300升。畜牧业还产生大量甲烷排放。为畜牧饲料扩展农业用地是森林砍伐的主要驱动因素。

食物浪费是另一个关键问题。全球生产的所有食物中约三分之一被损失或浪费。当食物最终进入垃圾填埋场时，它会分解并释放甲烷。

解决方案包括转向植物性饮食、提高农业效率、减少食物浪费和开发更可持续的农业实践。

questions.question_text: What percentage of the world's land area does agriculture occupy?
questions.option_b: About 25%
questions.explanation: 文章指出农业占据约40%的世界土地面积。

questions.question_text: How much water is needed to produce one kilogram of beef?
questions.option_b: About 1,500 liters
questions.explanation: 文章指出生产一公斤牛肉需要约15000升水。

questions.question_text: What percentage of all food produced globally is lost or wasted?
questions.option_b: About one-quarter
questions.explanation: 文章指出全球生产的食物中约三分之一被损失或浪费。

questions.question_text: The word 'squandered' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Wasted or used inefficiently
questions.explanation: 'squandered'意为'浪费的、挥霍的'。

title: The Sharing Economy
content_en:
The sharing economy has disrupted traditional business models across numerous industries. Platforms like Uber, Airbnb, and TaskRabbit connect people who have underutilized assets or skills with those who need them, creating new economic opportunities while challenging established industries.

Proponents argue that the sharing economy promotes more efficient use of resources. Spare rooms, idle vehicles, and free time are transformed into income-generating assets. Consumers benefit from lower prices and greater convenience, while providers gain flexible earning opportunities.

However, critics raise serious concerns. Workers in the gig economy often lack the protections afforded to traditional employees, including minimum wage guarantees, health insurance, and retirement benefits. The classification of workers as independent contractors rather than employees has been the subject of numerous legal battles.

The impact on traditional businesses has been significant. Taxi companies, hotels, and other established industries have struggled to compete with sharing economy platforms that operate under different regulatory frameworks. Cities around the world are grappling with how to regulate these platforms to protect consumers and workers while preserving the benefits of innovation.
content_cn:
共享经济颠覆了众多行业的传统商业模式。优步、爱彼迎等平台将拥有未充分利用资产或技能的人与需要这些的人连接起来。

支持者认为共享经济促进了更高效的资源利用。空余房间、闲置车辆和空闲时间被转化为创收资产。

然而，批评者提出了严重关切。零工经济中的工人通常缺乏传统员工享有的保护，包括最低工资保障、健康保险和退休福利。

对传统企业的影响是显著的。出租车公司、酒店等传统行业一直在努力与在不同监管框架下运营的共享经济平台竞争。

questions.question_text: What does the sharing economy do according to proponents?
questions.option_b: Promotes more efficient use of resources
questions.explanation: 文章指出支持者认为共享经济促进了更高效的资源利用。

questions.question_text: What concern do critics raise about gig economy workers?
questions.option_b: They lack protections like minimum wage guarantees and health insurance
questions.explanation: 文章指出零工经济工人缺乏传统员工享有的保护。

questions.question_text: How are workers in the sharing economy typically classified?
questions.option_b: As independent contractors
questions.explanation: 文章指出工人被分类为独立承包商而非员工。

questions.question_text: The word 'grappling' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Struggling to deal with
questions.explanation: 'grappling'意为'努力应对、挣扎处理'。

title: Globalization and Trade
content_en:
Globalization has transformed the world economy over the past several decades, creating unprecedented connections between countries through trade, investment, and the movement of people. International trade has grown dramatically, with the value of global merchandise exports increasing from about 300 billion dollars in 1970 to over 20 trillion dollars today.

The benefits of globalization are substantial. Consumers enjoy access to a wider variety of goods at lower prices. Developing countries have gained access to global markets, creating jobs and lifting millions out of poverty. The flow of ideas, technology, and culture across borders has accelerated innovation and human progress.

However, globalization has also created winners and losers. Manufacturing workers in developed countries have lost jobs as production moved to countries with lower labor costs. Income inequality has increased within many nations, even as global inequality has decreased. Environmental standards may be compromised as countries compete to attract investment.

The recent rise of protectionism and trade tensions challenges the globalization model. Some countries are imposing tariffs and trade barriers, arguing that free trade has benefited corporations at the expense of workers. Finding a balance between open trade and protecting domestic interests remains one of the most challenging economic policy questions of our time.
content_cn:
全球化在过去几十年中改变了世界经济，通过贸易、投资和人员流动创造了各国之间前所未有的联系。国际贸易急剧增长，全球商品出口价值从1970年的约3000亿美元增加到今天的超过20万亿美元。

全球化的好处是巨大的。消费者可以以更低的价格获得更丰富的商品。发展中国家获得了进入全球市场的机会，创造了就业机会。

然而，全球化也创造了赢家和输家。发达国家的制造业工人随着生产转移到劳动力成本较低的国家而失去了工作。许多国家内部的不平等加剧了。

最近保护主义和贸易紧张局势的兴起挑战了全球化模式。在开放贸易和保护国内利益之间找到平衡仍然是我们这个时代最具挑战性的经济政策问题之一。

questions.question_text: What was the value of global merchandise exports in 1970?
questions.option_b: About 300 billion dollars
questions.explanation: 文章指出1970年全球商品出口价值约3000亿美元。

questions.question_text: What benefit of globalization is mentioned for developing countries?
questions.option_a: They have gained access to global markets, creating jobs and reducing poverty
questions.option_b: They have eliminated all poverty
questions.option_c: They no longer need to trade
questions.option_d: They have the highest wages globally
questions.explanation: 文章指出发展中国家获得了全球市场准入，创造了就业和减少贫困。

questions.question_text: What challenge to the globalization model is mentioned?
questions.option_b: The rise of protectionism and trade tensions
questions.explanation: 文章指出保护主义和贸易紧张局势的兴起挑战了全球化模式。

questions.question_text: The word 'protectionism' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Government policies that restrict international trade to protect domestic industries
questions.explanation: 'protectionism'意为'保护主义'。

title: The Rise of E-commerce
content_en:
E-commerce has revolutionized the way people buy and sell goods and services. Online retail sales have grown exponentially, from virtually nothing in the 1990s to over 5 trillion dollars annually worldwide. This transformation has reshaped consumer behavior, business strategies, and the global economy.

The convenience of online shopping is a major driver of e-commerce growth. Consumers can browse and purchase products from anywhere at any time, compare prices across multiple retailers, and have items delivered directly to their doors. The COVID-19 pandemic dramatically accelerated this trend as lockdowns forced consumers to shop online.

Small businesses have both benefited from and been challenged by e-commerce. Online platforms allow small retailers to reach customers worldwide, but they also face intense competition from large online marketplaces. Independent sellers on platforms like Amazon must often compete with the platform's own products, creating an uneven playing field.

The growth of e-commerce has had significant impacts on traditional retail. Brick-and-mortar stores have closed in record numbers, and shopping malls face declining foot traffic. However, the line between online and offline retail is blurring as traditional retailers develop online presence and e-commerce companies open physical stores. The future of retail likely lies in omnichannel strategies that seamlessly integrate digital and physical shopping experiences.
content_cn:
电子商务已经彻底改变了人们买卖商品和服务的方式。在线零售额从1990年代几乎为零增长到全球每年超过5万亿美元。

在线购物的便利性是电子商务增长的主要驱动力。消费者可以随时随地浏览和购买产品。新冠疫情大大加速了这一趋势。

小企业既从电子商务中受益，也面临挑战。在线平台允许小型零售商接触全球客户，但它们也面临来自大型在线市场的激烈竞争。

电子商务的增长对传统零售产生了重大影响。实体店以创纪录的数量关闭。然而，在线和线下零售之间的界限正在模糊。零售的未来可能在于无缝整合数字和物理购物体验的全渠道策略。

questions.question_text: How much are annual global online retail sales?
questions.option_b: Over 5 trillion dollars
questions.explanation: 文章指出全球在线零售额每年超过5万亿美元。

questions.question_text: What accelerated the e-commerce trend?
questions.option_b: Decreased internet access
questions.explanation: 文章指出新冠疫情大大加速了电子商务趋势。

questions.question_text: What challenge do small businesses face on e-commerce platforms?
questions.option_b: Intense competition from large online marketplaces and the platform's own products
questions.explanation: 文章指出小企业面临来自大型在线市场和平台自身产品的激烈竞争。

questions.question_text: The word 'omnichannel' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Using all available channels seamlessly integrated together
questions.explanation: 'omnichannel'意为'全渠道的'。

title: Income Inequality
content_en:
Income inequality has become one of the defining economic issues of the twenty-first century. In many countries, the gap between the rich and the poor has widened significantly over the past several decades, raising concerns about social cohesion, economic stability, and democratic governance.

The statistics are striking. The richest 1 percent of the global population owns more wealth than the remaining 99 percent combined. In the United States, the top 10 percent of earners take home nearly half of all income, while the bottom 50 percent earns just 12 percent. This trend is not limited to developed countries; inequality has increased in most nations around the world.

Multiple factors contribute to rising inequality. Technological change has increased the demand for highly skilled workers while reducing opportunities for those with less education. Globalization has shifted manufacturing jobs to lower-wage countries. Declining union membership and minimum wages that have not kept pace with inflation have also contributed.

The consequences of extreme inequality are far-reaching. It can undermine social trust, increase political polarization, and reduce economic mobility. Children born into poverty face significant disadvantages in education and career opportunities, perpetuating inequality across generations. Addressing income inequality requires comprehensive policies including progressive taxation, investment in education, strengthened social safety nets, and policies that ensure workers share in economic growth.
content_cn:
收入不平等已成为二十一世纪最重要的经济问题之一。在许多国家，过去几十年来贫富差距显著扩大。

统计数据令人震惊。全球最富有的1%人口拥有的财富超过了其余99%的总和。在美国，收入最高的10%获得了近一半的收入，而底层的50%只获得12%。

多种因素导致不平等加剧。技术变革增加了对高技能工人的需求，同时减少了受教育程度较低者的机会。全球化将制造业工作转移到了低工资国家。工会成员减少和最低工资未跟上通胀也起了作用。

极端不平等的后果是深远的。它可能破坏社会信任，增加政治两极分化，并减少经济流动性。解决收入不平等需要包括累进税制、教育投资、加强社会安全网等全面政策。

questions.question_text: What percentage of global wealth does the richest 1% own?
questions.option_b: More than the remaining 99% combined
questions.option_c: About the same as the remaining 99%
questions.explanation: 文章指出最富有的1%拥有的财富超过了其余99%的总和。

questions.option_b: About 50%

questions.explanation: 'progressive taxation'意为'累进税制'，即税率随收入增加而提高。

title: Cryptocurrency and Digital Finance
content_en:
Cryptocurrency has emerged as one of the most disruptive innovations in the financial world. Since the creation of Bitcoin in 2009, thousands of cryptocurrencies have been launched, with a total market capitalization that has exceeded 2 trillion dollars at its peak.

Cryptocurrencies operate on blockchain technology, which provides a decentralized and transparent system for recording transactions. Unlike traditional currencies issued by central banks, cryptocurrencies are not controlled by any single entity. This decentralization is seen by proponents as a fundamental advantage, protecting against government manipulation and providing financial freedom.

However, cryptocurrencies face significant challenges. Price volatility makes them unreliable as stores of value or units of account. Regulatory uncertainty varies widely across jurisdictions, creating a patchwork of rules that complicates adoption. The environmental impact of energy-intensive mining processes, particularly for Bitcoin, has drawn criticism. Security concerns including hacking, fraud, and lost passwords have resulted in billions of dollars in losses.

Central bank digital currencies, or CBDCs, represent a government response to the cryptocurrency phenomenon. Unlike decentralized cryptocurrencies, CBDCs would be issued and controlled by central banks, combining the efficiency of digital payments with the stability of government-backed currency. The evolution of digital finance will likely reshape the global financial system in profound ways in the coming decades.
content_cn:
加密货币已成为金融界最具颠覆性的创新之一。自2009年比特币创建以来，已推出数千种加密货币，总市值在巅峰时期超过2万亿美元。

加密货币在区块链技术上运行，提供了一个去中心化和透明的交易记录系统。与传统货币不同，加密货币不受任何单一实体控制。

然而，加密货币面临重大挑战。价格波动使其作为价值储存或记账单位不可靠。能源密集型挖矿过程的环境影响受到了批评。安全问题包括黑客攻击和欺诈造成了数十亿美元的损失。

央行数字货币（CBDC）代表了政府对加密货币现象的回应。与去中心化加密货币不同，CBDC将由中央银行发行和控制。数字金融的演变可能在未来几十年以深刻的方式重塑全球金融体系。

questions.question_text: When was Bitcoin created?
questions.explanation: 文章指出比特币于2009年创建。

questions.question_text: What is the total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies at its peak?
questions.option_b: Over 2 trillion dollars
questions.explanation: 文章指出加密货币总市值在巅峰时期超过2万亿美元。

questions.question_text: What are CBDCs?
questions.option_b: Central bank digital currencies issued and controlled by central banks
questions.explanation: 文章指出CBDC是由中央银行发行和控制的央行数字货币。

questions.question_text: The word 'patchwork' most likely means:
questions.option_b: An inconsistent mixture of different things
questions.explanation: 'patchwork'意为'拼凑的东西'，指不一致的规则混合体。

title: The Gig Economy
content_en:
The gig economy has transformed the nature of work for millions of people around the world. Characterized by short-term contracts, freelance work, and independent contracting rather than permanent employment, the gig economy offers both flexibility and uncertainty.

Ride-sharing drivers, food delivery couriers, freelance writers, and online tutors are among the millions of workers who participate in the gig economy. Digital platforms connect these workers with customers, making it easier than ever to find short-term work. For many, the flexibility to set their own hours and choose their assignments is the primary attraction.

However, the gig economy comes with significant downsides. Workers typically lack benefits such as health insurance, paid leave, and retirement plans. Income can be unpredictable, and workers bear all the financial risk of slow periods. The absence of job security and career progression opportunities can create long-term financial instability.

The regulatory landscape for gig work is evolving rapidly. Some jurisdictions have enacted laws requiring platforms to provide minimum wage guarantees and benefits to workers. Others have maintained the independent contractor classification, preserving the flexibility that many workers value. The debate over how to balance flexibility with security is likely to continue as the gig economy grows and matures.
content_cn:
零工经济改变了全球数百万人的工作性质。零工经济以短期合同、自由职业和独立承包为特征，而非永久性就业，它既提供了灵活性也带来了不确定性。

网约车司机、外卖送餐员、自由撰稿人和在线教师是参与零工经济的数百万人中的一部分。数字平台将这些工人与客户联系起来。

然而，零工经济也有显著的缺点。工人通常缺乏健康保险、带薪休假和退休计划等福利。收入可能不可预测。

零工工作的监管环境正在快速演变。一些司法管辖区已颁布法律要求平台为工人提供最低工资保障和福利。

questions.question_text: What characterizes the gig economy?
questions.option_b: Short-term contracts, freelance work, and independent contracting
questions.explanation: 文章指出零工经济以短期合同、自由职业和独立承包为特征。

questions.question_text: What is the primary attraction of gig work for many workers?
questions.option_b: The flexibility to set their own hours and choose assignments
questions.explanation: 文章指出对许多人来说，设定自己的工作时间和选择任务的灵活性是主要吸引力。

questions.question_text: What downside of gig work is mentioned?
questions.option_b: Lack of benefits such as health insurance, paid leave, and retirement plans
questions.explanation: 文章指出零工工人通常缺乏健康保险、带薪休假和退休计划等福利。

questions.question_text: The word 'downsides' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Disadvantages or negative aspects
questions.explanation: 'downsides'意为'缺点、不利方面'。

title: Sustainable Business Practices
content_en:
Sustainable business practices, once considered a niche concern, have moved into the mainstream of corporate strategy. Companies around the world are increasingly recognizing that environmental and social responsibility is not just an ethical obligation but also a competitive advantage.

The concept of the triple bottom line, which measures business performance in terms of people, planet, and profit, has gained widespread acceptance. Companies that prioritize sustainability can attract environmentally conscious consumers, reduce operational costs through energy efficiency, and mitigate risks associated with environmental regulations.

Corporate sustainability reporting has become standard practice for large companies. Environmental, Social, and Governance criteria are increasingly used by investors to evaluate companies. Studies have shown that companies with strong ESG performance often deliver competitive financial returns over the long term.

However, greenwashing remains a concern. Some companies exaggerate or fabricate their environmental credentials to appeal to conscious consumers without making meaningful changes to their practices. Independent verification and standardized reporting frameworks are helping to address this issue. Despite challenges, the trend toward sustainable business practices continues to accelerate, driven by consumer demand, regulatory pressure, and the recognition that sustainable practices are essential for long-term business success.
content_cn:
可持续商业实践曾经被视为小众关注，现在已进入企业战略的主流。全球各地的公司越来越认识到环境和社会责任不仅是道德义务，也是竞争优势。

三重底线概念（人、地球和利润）已获得广泛接受。优先考虑可持续性的公司可以吸引环保意识消费者，通过节能降低运营成本。

企业可持续发展报告已成为大公司的标准做法。环境、社会和治理标准被投资者越来越多地用来评估公司。

然而，漂绿仍然是一个担忧。一些公司夸大或伪造其环保资质。独立验证和标准化报告框架正在帮助解决这个问题。

questions.question_text: What is the triple bottom line?
questions.option_b: Measuring business performance in terms of people, planet, and profit
questions.explanation: 文章指出三重底线从人、地球和利润三个方面衡量企业绩效。

questions.question_text: What does ESG stand for?
questions.option_b: Environmental, Social, and Governance
questions.explanation: 文章指出ESG代表环境、社会和治理。

questions.question_text: What is greenwashing?
questions.option_b: Exaggerating or fabricating environmental credentials without meaningful changes
questions.explanation: 文章指出漂绿是指夸大或伪造环保资质而不做实质性改变。

questions.question_text: The word 'niche' most likely means:
questions.option_b: Specialized and limited
questions.explanation: 'niche'意为'小众的、专门的'。

title: The Housing Crisis
content_en:
Housing affordability has become a crisis in many major cities around the world. As property prices and rents have soared far beyond wage growth, an increasing number of people are struggling to find adequate and affordable housing.

The causes of the housing crisis are complex and multifaceted. In many cities, restrictive zoning laws limit the construction of new housing, creating artificial scarcity. Speculative investment in real estate drives up prices, treating homes as financial assets rather than places to live. Construction costs have risen significantly, and the pace of new building has not kept up with population growth in many urban areas.

The consequences are severe. Young adults are increasingly unable to leave their parents' homes, delaying independence and family formation. Low-income families may spend more than half their income on rent, leaving little for other necessities. Homelessness is rising in many cities, with devastating effects on individuals and communities.

Solutions include reforming zoning laws to allow more housing construction, implementing rent control or stabilization measures, increasing social housing investment, and taxing speculative property investment. Some cities are experimenting with innovative approaches such as community land trusts and cooperative housing models. Addressing the housing crisis requires coordinated action across government, private sector, and community organizations.
content_cn:
住房可负担性已成为全球许多主要城市的危机。随着房价和租金远远超过工资增长，越来越多的人难以找到适当和负担得起的住房。

住房危机的原因是复杂多样的。在许多城市，限制性分区法规限制了新住房建设，造成了人为稀缺。房地产投机投资推高了价格。建筑成本显著上升。

后果是严重的。年轻人越来越无法离开父母的家园。低收入家庭可能将一半以上的收入花在租金上。许多城市的无家可归者人数正在上升。

解决方案包括改革分区法规、实施租金控制、增加社会住房投资和对投机性房地产投资征税。

questions.question_text: What is a cause of the housing crisis?
questions.option_b: Restrictive zoning laws limiting new housing construction
questions.explanation: 文章指出限制性分区法规限制了新住房建设，造成人为稀缺。

questions.question_text: What may low-income families spend on rent?
questions.option_b: About 25% of income
questions.explanation: 文章指出低收入家庭可能将一半以上收入花在租金上。

questions.question_text: What is a community land trust?

title: The Minimum Wage Debate
content_en:
The debate over minimum wage has intensified in recent years as income inequality has grown and the cost of living has risen. Advocates argue that higher minimum wages are necessary to ensure workers can meet their basic needs, while opponents contend that significant increases could lead to job losses and economic harm.

Proponents of raising the minimum wage point to the growing gap between productivity and wages. Since the 1970s, worker productivity has increased significantly, but the federal minimum wage in the United States has not kept pace with inflation. A full-time worker earning the federal minimum wage lives below the poverty line in most states.

Opponents argue that significant minimum wage increases could force businesses to reduce hiring, cut hours, or automate jobs. Small businesses with thin profit margins may be particularly vulnerable. Some economists also warn that higher wages could lead to inflation as businesses pass increased labor costs on to consumers.

Research on the actual effects of minimum wage increases has produced mixed results. Some studies find minimal impact on employment, while others identify negative effects, particularly for young and low-skilled workers. Many cities and states have implemented higher minimum wages than the federal level, providing natural experiments for studying the effects. The debate continues as policymakers seek to balance the goal of fair wages with economic competitiveness.
content_cn:
随着收入不平等加剧和生活成本上升，最低工资辩论日益激烈。倡导者认为更高的最低工资对确保工人能够满足基本需求是必要的，而反对者则认为大幅增加可能导致失业和经济损害。

提高最低工资的支持者指出生产率和工资之间日益增长的差距。自1970年代以来，工人生产率显著提高，但联邦最低工资未能跟上通胀。

反对者认为大幅提高最低工资可能迫使企业减少招聘、削减工时或自动化工作。利润微薄的小企业可能特别脆弱。

关于最低工资提高实际影响的研究结果不一。一些研究发现对就业影响极小，而另一些则确定了负面影响，特别是对年轻人和低技能工人。

questions.option_b: It has grown significantly
questions.explanation: 文章指出自1970年代以来生产率和工资之间的差距日益增长。

questions.option_b: Below the poverty line in most states

questions.option_b: Consistent negative effects

title: Intellectual Property in the Digital Age
content_en:
Intellectual property, including patents, copyrights, trademarks, and trade secrets, has become increasingly important in the knowledge-based economy. As digital technology makes it easier to copy and distribute creative works, protecting intellectual property rights while promoting innovation has become a complex challenge.

Copyright protection in the digital age faces particular difficulties. Music, movies, books, and software can be copied and shared with millions of people at virtually no cost. While digital rights management technologies attempt to prevent unauthorized copying, they have had limited success. Streaming services have emerged as a legal and convenient alternative to piracy, though they provide significantly less revenue per unit to creators than traditional sales.

Patent systems are also under strain. The number of patent applications has skyrocketed, and critics argue that many granted patents are overly broad or obvious, stifling rather than promoting innovation. Patent trolls, companies that acquire patents primarily to extract licensing fees through litigation, have become a significant problem.

International intellectual property disputes have increased as the global economy becomes more interconnected. Developing countries argue that strict intellectual property protections favor wealthy nations and corporations, limiting access to essential medicines and technologies. Finding a balance that protects creators' rights while ensuring broad access to knowledge and innovation remains an ongoing challenge.
content_cn:
知识产权，包括专利、版权、商标和商业秘密，在知识经济中变得越来越重要。随着数字技术使复制和分发创意作品变得更加容易，在保护知识产权权利的同时促进创新已成为一个复杂的挑战。

数字时代的版权保护面临特殊困难。音乐、电影、书籍和软件几乎可以零成本复制和分享给数百万人。流媒体服务已作为合法便捷的盗版替代方案出现。

专利制度也面临压力。专利申请数量激增，批评者认为许多授予的专利过于宽泛或显而易见，反而扼杀而非促进创新。专利流氓已成为一个重大问题。

国际知识产权争端随着全球经济更加互联而增加。发展中国家认为严格的知识产权保护偏袒富裕国家和公司。

questions.option_b: Patents, copyrights, trademarks, and trade secrets
questions.explanation: 文章指出知识产权包括专利、版权、商标和商业秘密。

questions.option_b: Provided a legal and convenient alternative to piracy
questions.explanation: 文章指出流媒体服务作为合法便捷的盗版替代方案出现。

questions.option_b: Strict protections favor wealthy nations and limit access to medicines and technologies

title: The Future of Work
content_en:
The nature of work is undergoing a profound transformation driven by technological advancement, demographic shifts, and changing social expectations. Automation, artificial intelligence, and remote work are reshaping industries and redefining what it means to have a career.

Automation poses both opportunities and challenges. While it eliminates routine and dangerous tasks, it also threatens jobs in manufacturing, transportation, and administrative fields. Some estimates suggest that up to 30 percent of current jobs could be automated by 2030. However, history shows that technological change typically creates new types of jobs even as it eliminates others.

Remote work, accelerated by the pandemic, has become a permanent fixture for many organizations. This shift offers benefits including reduced commuting time, greater geographic flexibility, and improved work-life balance. However, it also presents challenges such as social isolation, blurred boundaries between work and personal life, and reduced opportunities for spontaneous collaboration.

The skills required in the future workplace are evolving. Critical thinking, creativity, emotional intelligence, and adaptability are becoming more valuable than technical knowledge that can quickly become outdated. Lifelong learning and continuous skill development will be essential for workers to remain competitive in a rapidly changing labor market. Organizations and governments must invest in education and training programs to prepare the workforce for the jobs of tomorrow.
content_cn:
工作的性质正在经历由技术进步、人口结构变化和不断变化的社会期望驱动的深刻转变。自动化、人工智能和远程工作正在重塑行业。

自动化既带来机遇也带来挑战。虽然它消除了常规和危险的任务，但也威胁着制造业、交通和行政领域的工作。一些估计表明到2030年多达30%的现有工作可能被自动化。

由疫情加速的远程工作已成为许多组织的永久特征。这种转变提供了减少通勤时间、更大的地理灵活性和改善的工作与生活平衡等好处。

未来工作场所所需的技能正在演变。批判性思维、创造力、情商和适应能力变得比可能很快过时的技术知识更有价值。

questions.option_b: Reduced commuting time and greater geographic flexibility

questions.question_text: What skills are becoming more valuable in the future workplace?
questions.option_b: Critical thinking, creativity, emotional intelligence, and adaptability
questions.explanation: 文章指出批判性思维、创造力、情商和适应能力变得更有价值。

title: Economic Impact of Aging Populations
content_en:
Many countries around the world are experiencing significant demographic shifts as their populations age. declining birth rates and increasing life expectancy are creating societies where older adults make up a growing proportion of the population, with profound economic implications.

Japan is at the forefront of this trend, with nearly 30 percent of its population aged 65 or older. Many European countries and China face similar demographic trajectories. By 2050, the number of people aged 60 and above globally is expected to reach 2.1 billion, more than double the number in 2017.

The economic pressures of aging populations are substantial. Pension systems face mounting strain as fewer workers support more retirees. Healthcare costs rise disproportionately with age, putting pressure on public health budgets. Labor shortages may emerge as the working-age population shrinks, potentially slowing economic growth.

However, aging populations also present opportunities. Older adults represent a growing consumer market for healthcare, leisure, and adapted products and services. Many older adults wish to remain active in the workforce, and their experience and skills can be valuable assets. Some countries are raising retirement ages, encouraging later-life employment, and investing in automation and productivity improvements to offset the economic effects of population aging.
content_cn:
许多国家正在经历人口老龄化的重要人口结构变化。出生率下降和预期寿命增加正在创造老年人占人口比例越来越大的社会。

日本走在这一趋势的前列，近30%的人口年龄在65岁或以上。到2050年，全球60岁及以上人口预计将达到21亿。

人口老龄化的经济压力是巨大的。养老金系统面临越来越大的压力。医疗保健费用随年龄不成比例地上升。随着劳动年龄人口减少，可能出现劳动力短缺。

然而，老龄人口也带来了机遇。老年人代表了医疗保健、休闲和适应性产品服务的不断增长的消费市场。一些国家正在提高退休年龄、鼓励晚年就业和投资自动化。
